title: Rotate Array
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, rotate the array to the right by k steps. For example, if nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] and k = 3, the array should be rotated to [5, 6, 7, 1, 2, 3, 4].

examples.explanation: Rotating the array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] by 2 steps results in [4, 5, 1, 2, 3]. The last two elements come to the front.
For the array [-1, -100, 3, 99] rotated by 2 steps, we take [3, 99] from the end and place it at the front.
Rotating the array [1, 2] by 3 steps (which is effectively 1 step due to the length of the array) gives us [2, 1].

hints: Consider how the array elements move when rotated.
You can use a temporary array to store the result.
Think about using the modulo operator to simplify k.
In-place rotation could involve reversing parts of the array.
